prompt: Create a cinematic dark sacred-music illustration in a wide 16:9 aspect ratio. Show a solitary elderly organist seated in profile on the right side, playing a massive cathedral pipe organ. He has curly gray hair, a short beard, a somber expression, and wears a long black coat, with his hands on the manuals. Behind him are tall black organ pipes, rows of round white stop knobs, and a weathered wooden organ console. Place a large textured golden circular halo or moon-like disc behind his head and shoulders, partially intersected by vertical golden architectural strips, creating a sacred yet ambiguous aura. On the left side, depict a dim Gothic church interior far below with stone arches, candlelight, mourners, and exactly 1 funeral coffin in the aisle covered with white flowers, surrounded by a dark congregation. From the organ keys, paint luminous flowing musical energy: transparent golden-white ribbons, fine staff-like curves, sparkling particles, and delicate circular note-like glyphs sweeping leftward over the funeral scene. Use a moody palette of deep navy, charcoal black, antique gold, smoky gray, and candle amber. The style should blend painterly realism, matte painting, and subtle surrealism, with dramatic chiaroscuro, grainy texture, dust, aged plaster, and soft volumetric light. Keep the composition solemn, consoling, and paradoxically sacred; no readable text, no modern objects, no watermark.
